title: Duke Energy responds to constructive carbon plan decision by North Carolina Utilities Commission
text: CHARLOTTE, N.C. , Dec. 30, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Duke Energy issued the following statement in response to today's order by the North Carolina Utilities Commission (NCUC) on its carbon plan, an outcome prescribed by North Carolina state law (Energy Solutions for North Carolina, HB951).

title: Duke Energy resumes normal power operations
text: CHARLOTTE, N.C. , Dec. 26, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Duke Energy has met today's expected peak energy demand in the Carolinas, thanks in part to customer efforts to conserve power after the weekend's arctic blast affecting power generation and delivery in much of the country.

title: Duke Energy thanks customers for energy conservation that helped keep the power on today
text: Company asks for continued energy conservation in the Carolinas through Monday morning to prevent possible rotating outages CHARLOTTE, N.C. , Dec. 25, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Duke Energy is crediting its customers' energy conservation with helping keep the lights on for everyone on Christmas Day and is asking customers to continue implementing simple conservation measures until 10 a.m.

title: Duke Energy asks for continued energy conservation as power restoration continues following extreme winter temperatures
text: Saturday's rotating outages to protect overall energy grid concluded Crews continue to restore power from Friday's winter weather CHARLOTTE, N.C. , Dec. 24, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Duke Energy asked customers to conserve energy usage as crews restored power following rotating outages in the Carolinas in response to high-energy demand Saturday morning.

title: Media Statement: Duke Energy curtailing energy load in the Carolinas
text: CHARLOTTE, N.C. , Dec. 24, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Due to the extreme cold temperatures and subsequent demand for power around much of the nation, electricity supplies are very tight.

title: Duke Energy Carolinas asks customers to voluntarily reduce electric usage
text: CHARLOTTE, N.C. , Dec. 24, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- As extreme temperatures drive unusually high energy demand across the Carolinas, Duke Energy is requesting customers to reduce their energy use for the next 24-hours.

title: Duke Energy prepares for winter storm and urges customers to do the same
text: Mix of below-freezing temperatures and high winds are expected to cause power outages Thursday and Friday Duke Energy lineworkers, damage assessors and vegetation crews are ready to respond Customers are encouraged to prepare in advance for extended outages and check on loved ones CINCINNATI , Dec. 21, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Duke Energy is monitoring and preparing for a winter storm system that is expected to cause power outages across southwest Ohio and Northern Kentucky this holiday weekend. A mix of dangerously low temperatures, high winds and snow is predicted to move across the region beginning late Thursday evening and continuing through Friday evening.

title: Duke Energy prepares for severe winter weather and urges customers to do the same
text: Mix of rain and high winds could cause power outages as below-freezing temperatures move into the region Lineworkers, damage assessors and vegetation crews are ready to respond Customers are encouraged to prepare in advance for extended outages and check on loved ones CHARLOTTE, N.C. , Dec. 21, 2022  /PRNewswire/ -- Duke Energy is monitoring and preparing for a winter weather system that may cause power outages across the Carolinas this holiday weekend.

title: Duke Energy supports North Carolina communities with more than $750,000 in grants to support emergency preparedness
text: Funding will support 24 nonprofits and government agencies from across the state Grants will pay for training, life-saving equipment and essential tools to aid in disaster planning and recovery operations RALEIGH, N.C. , Dec. 15, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- In the wake of another active storm season in the Carolinas, first responders and emergency managers are looking ahead to prepare their communities for the continued impacts of severe weather.

title: Duke Energy named one of North America's top sustainable companies for 17th straight year
text: CHARLOTTE, N.C. , Dec. 13, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- As Duke Energy continues to make significant progress in delivering the affordable, reliable and clean energy its customers and communities count on, the company was recently named to the Dow Jones Sustainability Index (DJSI) for North America for the 17th consecutive year.

title: Duke Energy completes restoration to all customers in Moore County and surrounding counties
text: Weekend attack disabled two local substations Restoration completed a day earlier than projected CHARLOTTE, N.C. , Dec. 8, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Duke Energy has restored power to all customers in Moore County and the surrounding counties affected by an attack on Saturday on two major substations.

title: More than 10,000 customers restored as Duke Energy continues progress in Moore County
text: Company to restore most customers by late Wednesday night Duke Energy Foundation makes $100,000 donation to local organizations CHARLOTTE, N.C. , Dec. 6, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- With around 10,000 customers restored so far, Duke Energy anticipates having nearly all customers restored by 11:59 p.m.

title: Duke Energy restores thousands of customers after substation vandalism in N.C.
text: CHARLOTTE, N.C. , Dec. 5, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Duke Energy crews have restored power to nearly 7,000 customers since Sunday night when substations in Moore County were attacked.

title: Duke Energy's chief operating officer Dhiaa Jamil to retire in 2023
text: Chief Generation Officer Preston Gillespie to expand role and join Senior Management Committee EVP Harry Sideris to add transmission organization to his responsibilities CHARLOTTE, N.C. , Dec. 5, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Duke Energy (NYSE: DUK) today announced that Dhiaa Jamil, executive vice president and chief operating officer, will retire midyear 2023 after a career spanning more than 41 years at the company.
